articles.title: Instructure confirms data breach, ShinyHunters claims attack - BleepingComputer
articles.description: Educational tech giant Instructure has confirmed that data was stolen in a cyberattack, with the ShinyHunters extortion gang claiming responsibility.

articles.title: Leaker claims Dark Souls trilogy remake in development - My Nintendo News
articles.description: Shirrako, a leaker with a relatively unknown track record, has stated on X that Bandai Namco is preparing a Dark Souls trilogy remake for undisclosed platforms. A release window was not reported…

articles.description: Google has been working on a Gemini app redesign for the past few months, and we now have our first look at the full overhaul.

articles.title: Valve: 67.74% of Steam users run Windows 11 - Neowin
articles.description: Valve has released the latest Steam Hardware & Software Survey data, showing Windows 11's continuous climb among the gaming audience.

articles.title: Microsoft Defender wrongly flags DigiCert certs as Trojan:Win32/Cerdigent.A!dha - BleepingComputer
articles.description: Microsoft Defender is detecting legitimate DigiCert root certificates as Trojan:Win32/Cerdigent.A!dha, resulting in widespread false-positive alerts, and in some cases, removing certificates from Windows.

articles.title: Modern Warfare 4 Being Playtested on PS4, It’s Claimed - Insider Gaming
articles.description: A prominent Call of Duty player on social media has claimed to have heard updates about Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4.

articles.description: AMD's next-generation Halo flagship, the Ryzen AI MAX+ 495, has leaked out, featuring a 10% bump in performance & a new Radeon 8065 iGPU.

articles.title: Short and tall people are the same height at this party — just for the night - CNN
articles.description: An Oakland same-height party used custom foam platforms to level guests, leaving people looking at each other eye to eye.

articles.title: Registry hack lets you bypass Windows 11 KB5083769, KB5083631 patch that blocks some apps - Neowin
articles.description: Microsoft earlier confirmed that its latest Windows 11 updates under KB5083769 and KB5083631 have new entries in its blocklist. A Registry fix can unpatch it.
